title: hq-package.yaml
description: Manifest schema for HQ packages — versioned bundles of workers, commands, skills, and knowledge installable via hq install.

name.description: Package slug — lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens. Used as the registry identifier and install target directory name.

type.description: Package type. Controls install target routing for all exposes entries.

version.description: Semver version string for this release.

minHQVersion.description: Minimum HQ version required to install this package. Installer aborts if installed HQ version is below this value.

description.description: Short human-readable description of what this package provides.

author.description: Author name or organization that created and maintains this package.

repo.description: Git repository URL for the package source. Used as offline fallback when the registry is unavailable.

requires.description: External dependencies this package needs at runtime.

packages.description: HQ package slugs this package depends on. Installed before this package.

services.description: Service slugs required at runtime. Installer warns if not configured in the target HQ installation.

exposes.description: Declares what this package installs into the HQ workspace. At least one sub-array must be non-empty.

workers.description: Paths within the tarball to worker.yaml files to install.

commands.description: Paths within the tarball to command .md files to install.

skills.description: Paths within the tarball to skill .md files to install.

knowledge.description: Paths within the tarball to knowledge directories or files to install.

hooks.description: Shell scripts run at install lifecycle events. Each value is a path within the tarball to an executable shell script.

on-install.description: Path to shell script run after all files are installed.

on-update.description: Path to shell script run after an existing installation is updated.

on-remove.description: Path to shell script run before files are removed from the HQ workspace.
